text.title: ai-plugin-ecosystem Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ai-plugin-ecosystem as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Assuming REST write access can create new frames.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Figma REST API write access is restricted: agents cannot create new frames, only update existing node properties. Design-from-scratch automation requires a human setup step.

failure-mode.description: Fetching the full file JSON for large files.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: File JSON exports can be 10–200 MB for complex files. Always scope requests to specific node IDs with the `?ids=node_id` parameter to avoid memory issues and rate limit exhaustion.

failure-mode.description: Using a read-only Figma token for write operations.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A read-only token silently fails write operations. Token scopes (read vs. write) must be set correctly per operation type.

failure-mode.description: Logging plugin version alongside design outputs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: AI plugin outputs change with model updates. Without version logging, it is impossible to reproduce or audit which model state produced a given design artifact.
